symbols: ruins — the transience of earthly empires and human works
camel and rider — pilgrimage and sojourning in a foreign land
dome and minaret — the historical overlay of faiths upon the Holy Land
solitary figure amid ruins — the smallness of humanity before the weight of history
description: A sunlit view of a partially ruined mosque or tomb complex in Cairo. A large domed structure with an ornate minaret rises at left, its walls crumbling and weathered. To the right, a stone wall with a large pointed archway frames a passage. A lone figure rides a camel across the dusty open ground between the buildings. Another small figure stands near the ruined wall at far left. The sky is bright blue with scattered white clouds.

scripture_refs.reason: Egypt and Cairo evoke the biblical motif of sojourning in Egypt, beginning with Abraham's descent during famine
Oracle concerning Egypt — the LORD rides on a swift cloud and comes to Egypt, and the idols of Egypt tremble
The solitary pilgrim amid ancient ruins evokes the theme of strangers and pilgrims on earth seeking a better country
